title: CIL Civil — Advanced Mock 13 (Conceptual)
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: As per Income Tax Department data (June 2026), India's net direct tax collections in early FY27 rose by about what percentage year-on-year?
questions.solution: Net direct tax collections rose ~14.6% YoY to about Rs 5.21 trillion.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, which country became the first Central Asian member of the New Development Bank (NDB)?
questions.solution: Uzbekistan became the first Central Asian nation to join the BRICS-backed NDB.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which India-born theoretical physicist won the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics?
questions.solution: Rajasthan-born physicist Jainendra K. Jain received the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which institution ranked first in the QS World University Rankings 2027 (released June 2026)?
questions.solution: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) ranked first in QS World University Rankings 2027.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In June 2026, which Indian jurist was elected a Judge of the International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea (ITLOS)?
questions.solution: Indian jurist Bimal N. Patel was elected to ITLOS in June 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, PM Narendra Modi received the highest national award of which country?
questions.solution: PM Modi was conferred Slovakia's highest national award in 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026, in which an Indian Army contingent participated, was held in which country?
questions.solution: The multinational Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026 was held in Mongolia.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who was named the new Chairman of GIC Re in June 2026?
questions.solution: The government named Hitesh Joshi as the new Chairman of GIC Re.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, ONGC was assigned to develop a new strategic petroleum reserve (SPR) facility at which location?
questions.solution: ONGC was tasked with building a new underground crude oil SPR cavern at Mangaluru, Karnataka.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The India–UK CETA and Social Security Agreement were reported to take effect from which date?
questions.solution: Reports indicated the India–UK CETA would take effect from July 15, 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which vitamin is produced by the human body on exposure to sunlight?
questions.solution: Sunlight triggers Vitamin D synthesis in the skin.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The 'Maharatna' status was granted to Coal India Limited in which decade?
questions.solution: CIL was conferred Maharatna status in 2011 (the 2010s).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The largest coalfield in India by reserves is the:
questions.solution: Jharia in Jharkhand holds major prime coking coal reserves.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which Indian state has the longest coastline?
questions.solution: Gujarat has the longest coastline among Indian states.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The headquarters of Coal India Limited (CIL) is located in which city?
questions.solution: Coal India Limited is headquartered in Kolkata, West Bengal.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is the largest coal-producing state in India in recent years?
questions.solution: Chhattisgarh has been India's largest coal-producing state, ahead of Odisha and Jharkhand.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Coal India Limited operates under which Union Ministry?
questions.solution: CIL functions under the Ministry of Coal, Government of India.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which subsidiary of CIL is the single largest coal-producing company?
questions.solution: South Eastern Coalfields Limited (SECL) is among CIL's largest producing subsidiaries.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the capital of Australia?
questions.solution: Canberra is the capital of Australia, not Sydney or Melbourne.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the capital of Canada?
questions.solution: Ottawa is the national capital of Canada.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Mount Kilimanjaro, the highest mountain in Africa, is located in which country?
questions.solution: Mount Kilimanjaro lies in north-eastern Tanzania.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is the largest planet in the Solar System?
questions.solution: Jupiter is the largest planet by both mass and diameter.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.solution: Iron oxide on its surface gives Mars a reddish appearance.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The SI unit of electric current is the:
questions.solution: The ampere (A) is the SI base unit of electric current.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which gas is most abundant in Earth's atmosphere?
questions.solution: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of the atmosphere by volume.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A invests Rs 5000 for 6 months and B invests Rs 6000 for 6 months. Out of a total profit of Rs 3000, A's share is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Profit is split in the ratio of capital × time invested by each partner.
**Formula.** $\text{share}_A=P\cdot\tfrac{x\,m_A}{x\,m_A+y\,m_B}$
**Given.** A: Rs 5000×6 mo; B: Rs 6000×6 mo; profit Rs 3000.
**Working.** Ratio $=30000:36000$; share$_A=3000\times\tfrac{30000}{66000}$.
**Answer.** A's share $=$ Rs 1364.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Two trains 140 m and 100 m long move in opposite directions at 72 km/h and 36 km/h. The time to cross each other is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Opposite directions add speeds; convert km/h to m/s with the factor 5/18, then divide the combined length by the relative speed.
**Formula.** $t=\tfrac{L_1+L_2}{(s_1+s_2)\times\frac{5}{18}}$
**Given.** L₁ = 140 m, L₂ = 100 m, s₁ = 72 km/h, s₂ = 36 km/h.
**Working.** Relative $=(72+36)\times\tfrac{5}{18}=30$ m/s; $t=\tfrac{140+100}{30}$.
**Answer.** $t=8$ s.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The difference between compound and simple interest on Rs 8000 at 5% per annum for 2 years is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Over 2 years the gap between compound and simple interest is the first year's interest-on-interest.
**Formula.** $CI-SI=P\left(\tfrac{r}{100}\right)^2$
**Given.** P = Rs 8000, r = 5% p.a., t = 2 yr.
**Working.** $=8000\left(\tfrac{5}{100}\right)^2$.
**Answer.** $CI-SI=$ Rs 20.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A pipe fills a tank in 8 h, but with a leak it takes 10 h. The leak alone can empty the full tank in:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The leak's rate is the fill-rate without the leak minus the slower fill-rate with the leak.
**Formula.** $T=\tfrac{ab}{b-a}$
**Given.** Fill without leak a = 8 h; with leak b = 10 h.
**Working.** Leak rate $=\tfrac1{8}-\tfrac1{10}$; emptying time $=\tfrac{8\times10}{10-8}$.
**Answer.** $T=40$ h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at 7:20 is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The hour hand sweeps 30° per hour and 0.5° per minute, the minute hand 6° per minute; take the smaller of the two angles.
**Formula.** $\theta=\left|30H-5.5M\right|$
**Given.** H = 7, M = 20.
**Working.** $=\left|30\times7-5.5\times20\right|=100^\circ$; smaller $=\min(100,360-100)$.
**Answer.** $\theta=100^\circ$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A vessel holds 100 L of milk. 10 L is drawn out and replaced with water; this is repeated 3 times. The milk remaining is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each draw-and-replace keeps the fraction $(1-x/V)$ of milk; after n repeats this fraction is raised to the n-th power.
**Formula.** $M=V\left(1-\tfrac{x}{V}\right)^{n}$
**Given.** V = 100 L, x = 10 L, n = 3.
**Working.** $=100\left(1-\tfrac{10}{100}\right)^{3}$.
**Answer.** $M\approx 72.9$ L.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A boat covers 221 km downstream in 13 h and the same 221 km upstream in 17 h. Its speed in still water is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Downstream speed adds the stream; upstream subtracts it. Still-water speed is the average of the two.
**Formula.** $u=\tfrac12\left[(b+s)+(b-s)\right]$
**Given.** Downstream 221 km in 13 h; upstream 221 km in 17 h.
**Working.** Downstream $=\tfrac{221}{13}=17$ km/h, upstream $=\tfrac{221}{17}=13$ km/h.
**Answer.** Still-water speed $u=\tfrac{17+13}{2}=15$ km/h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A trader marks goods 50% above cost, then gives a 20% discount. His net profit percentage is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Net selling factor is the markup factor times the discount factor; profit% is that factor reduced by 1.
**Formula.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{m}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{d}{100}\right)$
**Given.** Markup m = 50%, discount d = 20%.
**Working.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{50}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{20}{100}\right)=1.2$.
**Answer.** Profit $=(f-1)\times100=20\%$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A invests Rs 5000 for 12 months and B invests Rs 8000 for 10 months. Out of a total profit of Rs 4500, A's share is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Profit is split in the ratio of capital × time invested by each partner.
**Formula.** $\text{share}_A=P\cdot\tfrac{x\,m_A}{x\,m_A+y\,m_B}$
**Given.** A: Rs 5000×12 mo; B: Rs 8000×10 mo; profit Rs 4500.
**Working.** Ratio $=60000:80000$; share$_A=4500\times\tfrac{60000}{140000}$.
**Answer.** A's share $=$ Rs 1929.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The HCF of 45 and 75 is:
questions.solution: The highest common factor of 45 and 75 is 15.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: What is the average of the first 10 natural numbers?
questions.solution: Average of first n naturals = (n+1)/2 = (10+1)/2 = 5.5.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: An article sold at Rs 600 incurs a 25% loss. Its cost price is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Pipe A fills a tank in 8 h and pipe B in 3 h. Together they fill it in:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The LCM of 14 and 21 is:
questions.solution: The lowest common multiple of 14 and 21 is 42.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The compound ratio of 4 : 3 and 4 : 9 is:
questions.solution: Compound ratio = (4×4) : (3×9) = 16 : 27.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: What is 45% of 240?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Successive discounts of 15% and 20% on a marked price of Rs 1200 give a final price of:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The probability of getting a multiple of 3 on a single throw of a fair die is:
questions.solution: Count the favourable outcomes out of 6 equally likely faces to get 1/3.

questions.stem: Simple interest on Rs 4000 at 6% per annum for 2 years is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: 2 kg of rice at Rs 40/kg is mixed with 5 kg at Rs 80/kg. The average price per kg is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The square root of 7225 is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A can do a work in 18 days and B in 30 days. Working together they finish it in:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The percentage increase when a value rises from 60 to 75 is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: An item bought for Rs 600 is sold for Rs 750. The profit percentage is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A 120 m long train crosses a pole in 12 seconds. Its speed is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term in the series: 4, 9, 19, 39, 79, ?
questions.solution: Each term = previous × 2 + 1, so the next term = 79 × 2 + 1 = 159.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A = 1, B = 2, … , Z = 26, what is the sum of the letter values of 'GEM'?
questions.solution: Add the positional values of G, E, M to get 25.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A is the brother of B. B is the brother of C. C is the father of D. How is A related to D?
questions.solution: Tracing each relationship step by step gives: Uncle.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a certain code, each letter of a word is moved two steps forward in the English alphabet. The code for 'CAT' is:
questions.solution: Replace every letter by the one two places ahead: CAT → ECV.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: No A is B. All B are C. Which conclusion necessarily follows?
questions.solution: Applying the rules of categorical syllogism, the valid conclusion is: Some C are not A.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 6 km towards north, then turns east and walks 8 km. How far is he from the starting point?
questions.solution: Straight-line distance = √(6² + 8²) = √100 = 10 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A = 1, B = 2, … , Z = 26, what is the sum of the letter values of 'BID'?
questions.solution: Add the positional values of B, I, D to get 15.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.solution: Tracing each relationship step by step gives: Mother.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 9 km north, then 12 km east. His distance from the start is:
questions.solution: Resultant = √(9² + 12²) = 15 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A person facing south turns 90° to the left. Now they face:
questions.solution: Turning left from south leads to East.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 3, 6, 12, 21, ?
questions.solution: Differences grow by 3; adding the next difference gives 33.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: Y, W, U, S, ?
questions.solution: Letters decrease by 2 each time, so the next letter is Q.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: Rose, Lily, Lotus, Mango?
questions.solution: Mango belongs to a different category from the rest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: All pens are tools. All tools are useful. Which conclusion follows?
questions.solution: Both premises are universal affirmatives, so the chained conclusion 'All pens are useful' follows.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: GH, JK, MN, ?
questions.solution: Each pair skips one letter; after MN comes PQ.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a row of 24 people, X is 5th from the left. X's position from the right is:
questions.solution: Position from right = 24 − 5 + 1 = 20.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.solution: Each number is halved, so 12 → 6.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: 3 : 9 :: 5 : ? (pattern n : n^2)

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 5, 15, 45, 135, ?
questions.solution: Each term is multiplied by 3, so next = 135 × 3 = 405.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 27, 64, 100, 125?
questions.solution: The others are perfect cubes; 100 is not.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If each letter is replaced by the next letter, GOLD is coded as:
questions.solution: Shift each letter forward by one to get HPME.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 2, 7, 13, 15?
questions.solution: 2, 7, 13 are prime; 15 is composite.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which of these fractions is the largest?
questions.solution: Comparing decimal values, 4/5 is the largest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If 2 January is a Thursday, what day is 9 January?
questions.solution: 9 − 2 = 7 days, exactly one week, so it is again Thursday.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 16, 25, 36, 49, ?
questions.solution: These are perfect squares; next = 8² = 64.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One who studies stars and celestial bodies is an:
questions.solution: An 'astronomer' scientifically studies celestial bodies.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One word for 'a speech made without preparation':
questions.solution: An unprepared speech is delivered 'extempore'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: A place where birds are kept is called:
questions.solution: An 'aviary' houses birds; an apiary keeps bees.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'to break the ice' means:
questions.solution: 'Break the ice' means to initiate conversation in a tense or new setting.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'a piece of cake' means:
questions.solution: 'A piece of cake' means something very easy to do.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'once in a blue moon' means:
questions.solution: 'Once in a blue moon' means something happening very rarely.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'to bury the hatchet' means:
questions.solution: 'Bury the hatchet' means to end a quarrel and make peace.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'to let the cat out of the bag' means:
questions.solution: It means to accidentally reveal a secret.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The plural of 'Child' is:
questions.solution: 'Child' has the irregular plural 'children'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The plural of 'Mouse' is:
questions.solution: 'Mouse' has the irregular plural 'mice'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Neither he nor I ___ wrong.'
questions.solution: With 'neither...nor', the verb agrees with the nearer subject 'I', so 'am'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Each of the students ___ a book.'
questions.solution: 'Each' is singular, so it takes 'has'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He is ___ honest man.'
questions.solution: 'Honest' starts with a vowel sound, so 'an' is used.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'She plays ___ violin beautifully.'
questions.solution: Musical instruments take the definite article 'the'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Passive voice of 'John wrote the letter.' is:
options: The letter is written by John
The letter was written by John
questions.solution: Past simple active becomes 'was written' in the passive.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Passive voice of 'She is reading a book.' is:
options: A book was read by her
A book has been read by her
A book is being read by her
A book is read by her
questions.solution: Present continuous active becomes 'is being read' in the passive.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Spot the error: 'He / don't / like / coffee.'
questions.solution: Third-person singular needs 'doesn't', not 'don't'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Spot the error: 'One of the boys / are / playing / outside.'
questions.solution: 'One of the boys' is singular, so it requires 'is'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correct form: 'I ___ my homework already.'
questions.solution: 'Already' signals present perfect, so 'have done' is correct.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correct form: 'By next year, she ___ here for a decade.'
questions.solution: A completed action by a future point uses the future perfect 'will have worked'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the word that means the opposite of 'Artificial'.
questions.solution: 'Artificial' is opposite to 'natural'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correct meaning of the word 'Frugal'.
questions.solution: 'Frugal' means careful with money, i.e. economical.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Abundant'.
questions.solution: 'Abundant' means existing in large quantity, i.e. plentiful.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Brief'.
questions.solution: 'Brief' means short or concise.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Happy'.
questions.solution: 'Happy' is synonymous with 'joyful'.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a perfect plane truss, the relation between members m and joints j is:
questions.solution: A statically determinate (perfect) plane truss satisfies m = 2j − 3.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lateral ties in an RCC column primarily prevent:
questions.solution: Ties hold the main bars in position and prevent their lateral buckling.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 15 kPa and friction angle φ = 20°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 80 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 15 kPa, φ = 20°, σ = 80 kPa.
**Working.** $=15+80\times\tan 20^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=44.12$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mild-steel bar and a high-strength-steel bar of identical dimensions carry the same axial load within the elastic range. Which is correct?
options: Elongation cannot be compared without the yield strengths.
Both elongate by essentially the same amount.
questions.solution: Elastic elongation = PL/AE depends on Young's modulus, which is ~200 GPa for all steels regardless of strength grade, so the elongations are equal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a singly reinforced section (Fe415, M25 concrete, width 230 mm, effective depth 550 mm), the limiting moment of resistance (Mu,lim = 0.138 fck b d²) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a balanced (limiting) Fe415 section the moment of resistance is fixed by the coefficient 0.138 in $f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$.
**Formula.** $M_{u,lim}=0.138\,f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$
**Given.** f_ck = 25 MPa, b = 230 mm, d = 550 mm.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.138\times25\times230\times550^2}{10^6}$ kNm.
**Answer.** $M_{u,lim}\approx 240$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The characteristic compressive strength of M20 concrete is:
questions.solution: M20 denotes a characteristic cube strength of 20 MPa at 28 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As per IS 456, the maximum spacing of main bars in a slab is the smaller of:
questions.solution: Maximum spacing of main reinforcement is 3 times effective depth or 300 mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries a central point load of 10 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 10 kN, L = 6 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{10\times6}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=15$ kNm at mid-span.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As per IS 800, the maximum slenderness ratio for a load-bearing steel compression member is:
questions.solution: The limiting slenderness ratio for members carrying loads is 180.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rectangular channel 5 m wide carries 6 m³/s. The critical depth (yc = (q²/g)^{1/3}) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Critical depth in a rectangular channel depends only on the discharge per unit width q.
**Formula.** $y_c=\left(\tfrac{q^2}{g}\right)^{1/3}$
**Given.** Q = 6 m³/s, B = 5 m, g = 9.81 m/s².
**Working.** $q=\tfrac{6}{5}=1.2$ m²/s; $y_c=\left(\tfrac{1.2^2}{9.81}\right)^{1/3}$.
**Answer.** $y_c\approx 0.53$ m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most common index organism for faecal contamination of water is:
questions.solution: E. coli is used as the indicator of faecal pollution in water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Standard Penetration Test (SPT) gives the soil's:
questions.solution: The SPT reports the blow count N used to estimate relative density and strength.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 15 kPa and friction angle φ = 35°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 120 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 15 kPa, φ = 35°, σ = 120 kPa.
**Working.** $=15+120\times\tan 35^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=99.02$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A quick sand condition occurs when the effective stress becomes:
questions.solution: When upward seepage reduces effective stress to zero, the soil loses strength (quick condition).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 4 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 10 kN/m over its entire length. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a cantilever produces its maximum moment at the fixed support.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{2}$
**Given.** w = 10 kN/m, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{10\times4^2}{2}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=80$ kNm at the fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The characteristic yield strength of Fe415 reinforcing steel is:
questions.solution: Fe415 denotes a yield strength of 415 MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The ratio of maximum to average shear stress for a rectangular section is:
questions.solution: For a rectangle, maximum shear stress is 1.5 times the average.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The detention time of a tank of volume 200 m³ treating a flow of 25 m³/h is:
questions.solution: Detention time = Volume / Discharge = 200 / 25 = 8 hours.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The compound mainly responsible for early strength gain in cement is:
questions.solution: C3S hydrates rapidly and gives early strength to cement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The thermal stress developed in a fully restrained bar for a temperature rise ΔT is:
questions.solution: Restrained thermal stress = αEΔT, independent of length and area.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most economical rolled section for a steel beam in bending is the:
questions.solution: The I-section concentrates material in the flanges, giving high section modulus per unit weight.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The development length Ld of a bar is given by:
questions.solution: Ld = φσs/(4τbd), where φ is bar diameter and τbd is design bond stress.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If E = 100 GPa and Poisson's ratio = 0.25, the modulus of rigidity G is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Comparing a three-hinged and a two-hinged arch, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: A three-hinged arch is statically indeterminate to the first degree.
Support settlement induces no stress in a three-hinged arch.
A three-hinged arch is free of temperature stresses.
A two-hinged arch is indeterminate to the first degree.
questions.solution: A three-hinged arch is statically determinate (the third hinge supplies the extra equation), so calling it indeterminate is false.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 4 m clay layer (Cc = 0.3, e₀ = 1) under an initial effective stress of 100 kPa receives a 80 kPa increment. The primary consolidation settlement is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Primary consolidation of a normally consolidated clay follows the logarithmic compression law.
**Formula.** $S_c=\tfrac{C_c H}{1+e_0}\log_{10}\tfrac{\sigma_0+\Delta\sigma}{\sigma_0}$
**Given.** C_c = 0.3, H = 4 m, e₀ = 1, σ₀ = 100 kPa, Δσ = 80 kPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.3\times4000}{1+1}\log_{10}\tfrac{100+80}{100}$ mm.
**Answer.** $S_c\approx 153.2$ mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Soil with particle size less than 0.002 mm is classified as:
questions.solution: Particles finer than 0.002 mm are classified as clay.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shear stress distribution across a rectangular beam section is:
questions.solution: Transverse shear stress varies parabolically, peaking at the neutral axis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 8 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 5 kN/m over its full span. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a simply supported beam peaks at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{8}$
**Given.** w = 5 kN/m, L = 8 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{5\times8^2}{8}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=40$ kNm.

figure.caption: Simply supported beam — UDL

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a cantilever carrying a uniformly distributed load over its whole span, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: The bending moment is zero at the free end.
The shear force is maximum at the fixed end.
The bending moment is maximum at the free end.
The deflection is maximum at the free end.
questions.solution: Bending moment in such a cantilever is zero at the free end and maximum (hogging) at the fixed end — so the stated claim is false.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If the fore bearing of a line is 120°, its back bearing is:
questions.solution: Back bearing = fore bearing ± 180° = 300°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the slope-deflection method, the primary unknowns are the:
questions.solution: Slope-deflection treats the unknown joint displacements (rotations/translations) as primary unknowns.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The moment distribution method was developed by:
questions.solution: Hardy Cross developed the moment distribution method in 1930.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge over a rectangular weir (Cd = 0.62) of length 3 m under a head of 0.3 m is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Discharge over a sharp-crested rectangular weir varies with the 3/2 power of the head.
**Formula.** $Q=\tfrac23 C_d\sqrt{2g}\,L\,H^{3/2}$
**Given.** C_d = 0.62, L = 3 m, H = 0.3 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac23\times0.62\times\sqrt{2\times9.81}\times3\times0.3^{1.5}$.
**Answer.** $Q\approx 0.9$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Torsional shear stress in a circular shaft is given by:
questions.solution: The torsion formula is τ/r = T/J = Gθ/L.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge over a rectangular weir (Cd = 0.62) of length 2 m under a head of 0.4 m is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Discharge over a sharp-crested rectangular weir varies with the 3/2 power of the head.
**Formula.** $Q=\tfrac23 C_d\sqrt{2g}\,L\,H^{3/2}$
**Given.** C_d = 0.62, L = 2 m, H = 0.4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac23\times0.62\times\sqrt{2\times9.81}\times2\times0.4^{1.5}$.
**Answer.** $Q\approx 0.93$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a fixed beam and a simply supported beam of equal span carrying the same central load, which is correct?
options: The simply supported beam deflects less.
Deflection depends only on the load, not the supports.
questions.solution: End fixity raises stiffness; the fixed beam's central deflection is one-quarter that of the simply supported beam (PL³/192EI vs PL³/48EI).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The degree of static indeterminacy of a propped cantilever beam is:
questions.solution: A propped cantilever has one redundant reaction, so indeterminacy = 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a fixed beam with a central point load W over span L, the magnitude of the fixed-end moment is:
questions.solution: Fixed-end moment for a central point load is WL/8 at each support.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The sum of distribution factors at a rigid joint equals:
questions.solution: Distribution factors at any joint always sum to unity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A pin-ended column (E = 200 GPa, I = 16×10⁻⁶ m⁴, length 4 m) has an Euler buckling load of about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A pin-ended column buckles at the Euler critical load with effective length equal to the actual length.
**Formula.** $P_{cr}=\tfrac{\pi^2 E I}{L^2}$
**Given.** E = 200 GPa, I = 16×10⁻⁶ m⁴, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{\pi^2\times200\times10^9\times16\times10^{-6}}{4^2}$ N.
**Answer.** $P_{cr}\approx 1973.9$ kN.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The error due to the curvature of the earth in levelling is:
questions.solution: Earth's curvature makes distant points read lower; correction is +0.0785 d² m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a balanced section with Fe415 steel, the limiting neutral axis depth factor xu,max/d is:
questions.solution: IS 456 specifies xu,max/d = 0.48 for Fe415 steel.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If LL = 50% and PL = 20%, the plasticity index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As per IS 456, the minimum grade of concrete for reinforced concrete work is:
questions.solution: IS 456 specifies M20 as the minimum grade for RCC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a simply supported beam with a central point load of 24 kN, the maximum shear force is:
questions.solution: Each reaction = W/2 = 12 kN, which is the maximum shear force.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Terzaghi's one-dimensional theory deals with the:
questions.solution: Terzaghi's theory describes one-dimensional consolidation of saturated soils.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The carry-over factor for a prismatic member in moment distribution is:
questions.solution: For a prismatic member with a far fixed end the carry-over factor is 1/2.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of length 2 m carries a 10 kN load at its free end (E = 200 GPa, I = 16×10⁻⁶ m⁴). The tip deflection (WL³/3EI) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An end-loaded cantilever has tip deflection $W L^3/3EI$.
**Formula.** $\delta=\tfrac{W L^3}{3 E I}$
**Given.** W = 10 kN, L = 2 m, E = 200 GPa, I = 16×10⁻⁶ m⁴.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{10\times10^3\times2^3}{3\times200\times10^9\times16\times10^{-6}}$ m.
**Answer.** $\delta\approx 8.3$ mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A three-hinged arch is:
questions.solution: Three equilibrium equations plus the hinge condition make it determinate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The cross-sectional area of one 20 mm diameter bar is approximately:
questions.solution: Area = π/4 × 20² = 314 mm².

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Castigliano's first theorem gives:
questions.solution: The partial derivative of strain energy with respect to a load gives the deflection at that load.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The partial safety factor for steel in the limit state method (IS 456) is:
questions.solution: γm for reinforcing steel is 1.15 in limit state design.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries two equal 15 kN point loads placed 2 m from each support. The maximum bending moment (constant between the loads) is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Two equal symmetric loads give equal reactions; between the loads the bending moment is constant at $R\cdot a$.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=P\,a$
**Given.** P = 15 kN, a = 2 m, span = 6 m.
**Working.** Each reaction $=15$ kN; $M_{max}=15\times2$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=30$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Müller-Breslau principle is used to draw:
questions.solution: It states the influence line for a response equals the deflected shape from a unit displacement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The pressure at a depth of 15 m in water (ρ = 1000 kg/m³, g = 9.81 m/s²) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a fixed cross-sectional area, which shape of strut offers the greatest resistance to buckling?
questions.solution: Buckling load rises with the radius of gyration; spreading the same area to a large radius (a thin tube) maximises I and hence the critical load.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a simply supported beam under a central point load, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: The bending moment is maximum at midspan.
The maximum bending stress occurs at the neutral axis.
The deflection is maximum at midspan.
The shear force is zero at midspan.
questions.solution: Bending stress varies linearly with distance from the neutral axis — it is zero at the NA and maximum at the extreme fibre, so that statement is false.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The superelevation for a design speed of 100 km/h and radius 150 m (e = V²/127R) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The ratio of maximum to average shear stress for a solid circular cross-section is:
questions.solution: For a circle the factor is 4/3 (the 3/2 value belongs to a rectangle) — a common trap.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge through a section of area 3 m² with velocity 4 m/s is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In moment distribution, the carry-over factor to the far end of a prismatic member whose far end is a pin (simply supported) is:
questions.solution: The familiar 1/2 carry-over applies only to a far FIXED end; a far pinned end carries over zero moment.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kinematic indeterminacy of a structure equals the number of:
questions.solution: Kinematic indeterminacy = number of independent unknown joint displacements (degrees of freedom).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point the major and minor principal stresses are 120 MPa and 40 MPa. From Mohr's circle, the maximum in-plane shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The maximum in-plane shear stress equals the radius of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\tau_{max}=\tfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 120 MPa, σ₃ = 40 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{120-40}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\tau_{max}=40$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A weld used to join two overlapping plates is a:
questions.solution: Fillet welds are used at the edges of overlapping or lapped plates.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries a central point load of 30 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 30 kN, L = 6 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{30\times6}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=45$ kNm at mid-span.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 165" width="320" height="165" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="40" y1="55" x2="280" y2="55" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="3"/><polygon points="40,55 31,72 49,72" fill="#64748b"/><polygon points="280,55 271,72 289,72" fill="#64748b"/><line x1="160" y1="16" x2="160" y2="46" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><polygon points="160,53 154,42 166,42" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="160" y="12" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">W = 30 kN</text><text x="160" y="88" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#475569">span L = 6 m</text><polygon points="40,112 160,144 280,112" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.12)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="160" y="159" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">BMD — Mmax = 45 kNm</text></svg>
figure.caption: Simply supported beam — central point load

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The partial safety factor for concrete in the limit state method (IS 456) is:
questions.solution: γm for concrete is 1.5 in limit state design.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The phenomenon of sudden reduction in compressive strength of a slender column is called:
questions.solution: Slender compression members fail by elastic buckling before yielding.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a void ratio e = 0.5, the porosity n is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The partial safety factor for yielding of steel (γm0) in IS 800 limit state design is:
questions.solution: IS 800:2007 specifies γm0 = 1.10 for yielding.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The principal stresses at a point are 90 MPa and 30 MPa. The normal stress on the plane carrying the maximum shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** On the plane of maximum shear the normal stress equals the centre of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\sigma_n=\tfrac{\sigma_1+\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 90 MPa, σ₃ = 30 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{90+30}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_n=60$ MPa.

figure.caption: Centre of Mohr's circle = mean normal stress

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Compression steel in a doubly reinforced beam is provided when:
options: Mu exceeds the limiting moment Mu,lim
questions.solution: When the design moment exceeds the section's balanced (limiting) capacity, compression steel is added.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lug angles in a steel tension member are provided to:
options: Reduce the length of the end connection
questions.solution: Lug angles add connecting area so the gusset connection length is shortened.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RL of a benchmark is 150 m. With back sight 1.5 m and fore sight 1 m, the RL of the next point is:
questions.solution: RL = 150 + 1.5 − 1 = 150.5 m (rise and fall).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Standard Proctor test is used to determine:
options: Optimum moisture content and maximum dry density
questions.solution: The Proctor compaction test gives the OMC and maximum dry unit weight.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An axial load of 150 kN acts on a bar of cross-sectional area 800 mm². The direct stress is:
questions.solution: Stress = P/A = 150000 N / 800 mm² = 187.5 MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The degree of static indeterminacy of a simply supported beam is:
questions.solution: A simply supported beam is statically determinate (indeterminacy = 0).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The limit state method of design checks both:
questions.solution: Limit state design verifies the limit states of collapse (strength) and serviceability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The principal stresses at a point are 90 MPa and 10 MPa. The normal stress on the plane carrying the maximum shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** On the plane of maximum shear the normal stress equals the centre of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\sigma_n=\tfrac{\sigma_1+\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 90 MPa, σ₃ = 10 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{90+10}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_n=50$ MPa.

figure.caption: Centre of Mohr's circle = mean normal stress

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shape factor of a rectangular cross-section in plastic bending is:
questions.solution: Shape factor = plastic modulus / elastic section modulus = 1.5 for a rectangle.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point σx = 80 MPa, σy = 60 MPa and τxy = 30 MPa. The major principal stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The major principal stress is the centre of Mohr's circle plus its radius.
**Formula.** $\sigma_1=\tfrac{\sigma_x+\sigma_y}{2}+\sqrt{\left(\tfrac{\sigma_x-\sigma_y}{2}\right)^2+\tau_{xy}^2}$
**Given.** σx = 80 MPa, σy = 60 MPa, τxy = 30 MPa.
**Working.** Centre $=70$, radius $=31.62$; $\sigma_1=70+31.62$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_1=101.62$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Darcy's law states that the discharge velocity v equals:
questions.solution: Darcy's law: v = ki, where k is permeability and i is hydraulic gradient.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The degree of static indeterminacy of a single-bay, single-storey portal frame with both bases fixed is:
questions.solution: A fixed-base single-bay portal frame is indeterminate to the third degree.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 8 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 10 kN/m over its full span. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a simply supported beam peaks at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{8}$
**Given.** w = 10 kN/m, L = 8 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{10\times8^2}{8}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=80$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The modular ratio m in working stress design is taken as:
questions.solution: IS 456 gives the modular ratio as m = 280/(3σcbc).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Using Manning's formula with n = 0.015, hydraulic radius R = 1 m and slope S = 0.0009, the mean velocity is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Manning's equation gives mean velocity from roughness, hydraulic radius and bed slope.
**Formula.** $V=\tfrac1n R^{2/3} S^{1/2}$
**Given.** n = 0.015, R = 1 m, S = 0.0009.
**Working.** $=\tfrac1{0.015}\times1^{2/3}\times\sqrt{0.0009}$.
**Answer.** $V\approx 2$ m/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The plasticity index of a soil is:
questions.solution: Plasticity Index = Liquid Limit − Plastic Limit.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The hydraulic mean depth of a circular pipe running full of diameter D is:
questions.solution: For a full circular pipe, hydraulic radius R = A/P = D/4.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a singly reinforced section (Fe415, M25 concrete, width 250 mm, effective depth 450 mm), the limiting moment of resistance (Mu,lim = 0.138 fck b d²) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a balanced (limiting) Fe415 section the moment of resistance is fixed by the coefficient 0.138 in $f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$.
**Formula.** $M_{u,lim}=0.138\,f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$
**Given.** f_ck = 25 MPa, b = 250 mm, d = 450 mm.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.138\times25\times250\times450^2}{10^6}$ kNm.
**Answer.** $M_{u,lim}\approx 174.7$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bolts subjected to both shear and tension are checked for:
questions.solution: An interaction (combined) check is required when bolts carry shear and tension together.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever 4 m long carries a 10 kN load at its free end. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: Max BM at fixed end = W × L = 10 × 4 = 40 kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shear strength of a cohesionless soil is governed by:
questions.solution: For sands, shear strength = σ' tanφ (no cohesion).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The specific gravity of soil solids is typically about:
questions.solution: The specific gravity of most soil solids is around 2.65.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point the major and minor principal stresses are 150 MPa and 50 MPa. From Mohr's circle, the maximum in-plane shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The maximum in-plane shear stress equals the radius of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\tau_{max}=\tfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 150 MPa, σ₃ = 50 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{150-50}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\tau_{max}=50$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shape factor of a solid circular section in plastic bending is about:
questions.solution: For a solid circular section the shape factor is about 1.7 (16/3π).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The velocity head corresponding to a velocity of 3 m/s is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The classical bearing capacity theory for shallow footings was given by:
questions.solution: Terzaghi's bearing capacity theory is the standard for shallow foundations.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shape of the ideal transition curve used on highways is a:
questions.solution: An ideal transition curve is a spiral where radius varies inversely with length.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 5 m carries a central point load of 8 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: Max BM = WL/4 = 8 × 5 / 4 = 10 kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The relation between porosity n and void ratio e is:
questions.solution: By definition, n = e/(1+e).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a fixed beam carrying a UDL w over span L, the support (fixed-end) moment is:
questions.solution: Fixed-end moment for a UDL is wL²/12 at each support.
